title: 10 best herbal essential oils for eczema
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for eczema are plant-derived oils that help alleviate the symptoms of eczema, a chronic skin condition characterized by inflammation, dryness, and itching. These oils work by reducing inflammation, promoting skin hydration, and soothing the skin. The best herbal essential oils for eczema include Melaleuca alternifolia, Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Urtica dioica, Symphytum officinale, Lavandula angustifolia, Saponaria officinalis, Chamomilla recutita, Hypericum perforatum, and Echinacea purpurea. If left untreated, eczema can lead to painful rashes, sleep deprivation, and social isolation, significantly impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with eczema because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil contains calendic acid, a potent compound that soothes itchy skin, reduces redness, and calms irritated areas. It also possesses antimicrobial properties, which help prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate eczema. Furthermore, the oil's emollient and moisturizing effects leave the skin feeling soft, supple, and protected, providing long-lasting relief from the symptoms of eczema.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Calendula oil has antibacterial properties that help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to eczema flare-ups and infections.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Calendula oil help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema, promoting a calm and soothing environment for the skin.

Wound-healing: Calendula oil has been shown to accelerate wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue elasticity, and enhancing the production of new tissue, making it an effective treatment for eczema lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated skin.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are rich in fatty acids and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in eczema-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers and dry them using shade drying or air circulation for two weeks.
Mix one part dried calendula flowers with two parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks to allow for gentle extraction of calendula's active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and reserve the liquid.
Transfer the resulting infused oil to small glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator for up to six months using a dark glass bottle with tight lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing properties. The oil's calming scent can reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of eczema flares. Additionally, lavender's moisturizing effects help to hydrate dry, itchy skin, reducing redness and discomfort. By applying lavender essential oils topically or inhaling its aroma through steam inhalation, individuals with eczema can experience relief from symptoms such as itching, scratching, and skin irritation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender essential oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with eczema, promoting healthy skin.
Soothing and Calming Properties: The calming effects of lavender essential oil can help alleviate itching and discomfort caused by eczema, promoting relaxation and reducing stress-induced flare-ups.

Antimicrobial and Antifungal Properties: Lavender essential oil's antimicrobial and antifungal properties can help combat skin infections and reduce the risk of secondary bacterial infections that can exacerbate eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing and calming eczema symptoms.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain a high amount of linoleic acid, an omega-6 fatty acid that helps to moisturize and hydrate the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers at dawn when their oil content is highest using scissors and collecting in a clean cloth bag.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers for 7-10 days in a warm dark place away from direct sunlight or heat sources.
Steam distill the dried lavender flowers using a high-quality steam distillation apparatus with distilled water at 100°c for 90 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil from the distillate through a coffee filter paper into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.
Use the pure lavandula angustifolia essential oil immediately or dilute with carrier oils such as coconut or jojoba at a 2% concentration for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. It calms irritated skin, reducing redness and itching, which are common symptoms of eczema. The oil's gentle, non-irritating nature makes it an effective treatment for sensitive skin, allowing it to penetrate deep into the pores to alleviate discomfort and promote healing. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate eczema, providing a calming and restorative effect on the skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema, providing relief from discomfort.
Antioxidant: Chamomile's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms such as dryness and irritation.
Soothing and Calming: Chamomile essential oil's calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for eczema flare-ups, allowing the skin to heal more effectively.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Flowers: Contains apigenin, an flavonoid that has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers in the early morning after dew has dried.
Dry the fresh flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Blend one part of dried chamomile flowers with two parts of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking daily.
Strain and filter the resulting liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter before bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with eczema because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as citral and geraniol, soothe itchy skin, reduce redness and swelling, and combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate eczema. Additionally, lemon balm essential oil has a calming effect on the skin, reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which are key factors in managing eczema symptoms. Its topical application can bring much-needed relief to individuals suffering from this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of lemon balm essential oil help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms.
Calming and soothing: Lemon balm essential oil has a calming and soothing effect on the skin, which helps to reduce itching, irritation, and discomfort associated with eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots are used to create essential oil that soothes skin irritations and reduces inflammation, making it effective in alleviating eczema symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the lemon balm plant contain flavonoids and terpenes that help calm skin itching and reduce redness associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh melissa officinalis leaves from organic farms at dawn on warm sunny days.
Wash the harvested leaves with distilled water and gentle soap in cold running water for five minutes.
Steam distill the washed leaves using a steam distillation apparatus with a 10:1 ratio of leaves to water for six hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil using activated charcoal and diatomaceous earth within three days of distillation.
Store the filtered melissa officinalis essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with eczema because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The oil's sesquiterpenes also help to reduce redness and swelling, while its antifungal compounds prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate eczema symptoms. Additionally, curry plant essential oils' moisturizing effects help to lock in moisture and reduce dryness, a common trigger for eczema flare-ups. Overall, using curry plant essential oils topically can provide relief from itching, inflammation, and discomfort associated with eczema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric essential oil contains curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and itching associated with eczema.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants in Turmeric essential oil neutralize free radicals, protecting the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by environmental factors that can exacerbate eczema.
Immune-modulating: The essential oil's immunomodulatory properties help regulate the body's response to allergens and irritants, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy skin barrier function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the curry plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing skin irritations associated with eczema.

Buds: Curry plant buds are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to possess antiallergic and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to alleviate symptoms of eczema such as redness, itching, and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest helichrysum italicum leaves in abundance and dry them properly using air circulation or desiccant within 2 days.
Combine 100 grams of dried helichrysum italicum leaves with 500 milliliters of high-proof solvent like hexane or petroleum ether.
Steam distill the mixture for 4 hours at a temperature range of 60 to 80 degrees celsius and collect essential oil fraction.
Filter the collected essential oil through a layer of activated charcoal or cotton wool to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the resulting helichrysum italicum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with tight lid at room temperature for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and moisturizing properties. The gel inside the aloe leaf contains powerful compounds that soothe itchy skin, reduce redness, and calm inflammation. When applied topically, aloe oil creates a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and preventing dryness and irritation. Additionally, its natural antibacterial and antifungal properties help to combat infections that can exacerbate eczema symptoms, promoting healthy skin and reducing the risk of further flare-ups.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal aloe essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema, allowing for faster healing and relief.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of herbal aloe essential oil can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that often exacerbate eczema, preventing further irritation and infection.

Wound-healing: Herbal aloe essential oil has been shown to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue elasticity, and enhancing the growth of new skin cells, helping to restore healthy skin function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their soothing and calming properties, aloe leaves help reduce inflammation and irritation associated with eczema.
Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, aloe roots promote skin health and may help alleviate symptoms of eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves measuring 10-12 inches in length from mature plants.
Cut the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and blend them with 2 cups of distilled water for 30 minutes.
Steep the mixture for 24 hours at room temperature to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding the solids and retaining the herbal extract.
Transfer the herbal extract to a glass bottle and add 10% jojoba oil as a carrier oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander essential oils helps with eczema because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm itchy, irritated skin. It also possesses natural antiseptic and antibacterial properties that help to reduce infection and prevent further inflammation, allowing the skin to heal naturally. Additionally, germander oil has been shown to enhance skin elasticity and hydration, reducing dryness and flakiness associated with eczema. As a result, it provides fast and effective relief from the discomfort and distress of eczema.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Germander essential oil's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress, which can exacerbate inflammation and itching associated with eczema.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Germander essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe and calm redness, swelling, and itching caused by eczema.

Antimicrobial properties: Germander essential oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can contribute to the development of eczema and reduce the risk of secondary infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the germander plant contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing itchy and inflamed skin.
Barks: The bark of the germander plant is rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing in eczema-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh teucrium chamaedrys leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated and before peak sun intensity.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours to preserve their bioactive compounds.
Combine 200g of dried teucrium chamaedrys leaves with 1l of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks allowing the herbal material to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth and discard the solids then store the essential oil in amber glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sandalwood essential oils helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and soothing properties. It has been traditionally used to calm irritated skin and reduce redness, making it an excellent natural remedy for eczema sufferers. The oil's sesquiterpenes also help to promote collagen production, improving skin elasticity and reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. Additionally, sandalwood essential oils can help to balance the skin's natural pH, creating a barrier against allergens and irritants that can exacerbate eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sandalwood essential oil has antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Sandalwood essential oil, such as alpha-santalol, can help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema, promoting a faster healing process.

Soothing and moisturizing properties: Sandalwood essential oil has a calming and nourishing effect on the skin, helping to soothe irritated skin, lock in moisture, and reduce dryness and itchiness characteristic of eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sandalwood plant are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin.

Barks: The barks of the sandalwood tree contain a high concentration of alpha-santalol, a compound with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness and itching associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of santalum album at dawn when they are most potent for maximum therapeutic benefits.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content and preserve oil.
Combine 20g of dried santalum album leaves with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking daily, allowing the plant's natural oils to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard solids after 14 days, retaining the infused oil for use as an effective eczema remedy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange essential oils helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The oil's beta-pinene content reduces redness and swelling, while its limonene component fights off bacterial infections that can exacerbate the condition. Additionally, bitter orange essential oil's ability to regulate the skin's natural pH balance helps restore the protective barrier function of the skin, alleviating itching and discomfort associated with eczema.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Bitter orange essential oil's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and damage caused by eczema, reducing its symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in bitter orange essential oil can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antimicrobial property: Bitter orange essential oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat infections that may exacerbate or cause eczema, promoting a healthy skin environment and reducing the risk of secondary infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the bitter orange plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm irritated skin associated with eczema.
Buds: The buds of the bitter orange plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh citrus x aurantium peels in the morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Steam distill the peels for 30 minutes using a clevenger apparatus at atmospheric pressure.
Monitor and collect the essential oil as it separates from the water phase after 15 minutes of distillation.
Filter the collected oil through a coffee filter or paper towel within 24 hours to remove impurities.
Store the final citrus x aurantium essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It soothes irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with eczema flare-ups. The oil's antiseptic and antibacterial compounds also help to prevent infection and promote healing, further alleviating symptoms. Additionally, fennel essential oil's ability to improve digestion can aid in reducing eczema triggers such as food sensitivities, making it a holistic approach to managing the condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidant property of fennel essential oil helps to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to skin damage and exacerbate eczema symptoms.

Antimicrobial property: Fennel essential oil's antimicrobial property helps to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause or worsen eczema, promoting a healthy and balanced skin microbiome.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are used to create an essential oil that helps soothe and calm itchy skin, reducing inflammation associated with eczema.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling caused by eczema, making them a valuable ingredient in medicinal essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in the early morning when dew is still present on the plants for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested material using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius for 24 hours or until crispy and brittle texture achieved.
Steam distill the dried foeniculum vulgare material using a steam distillation apparatus with a yield of 10ml per hour and 1% essential oil content expected.
Filter the distillate through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and contaminants resulting in a clear yellowish liquid appearance.
Store the final essential oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months with no refrigeration required.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eczema with herbal essential oils is by blending Lavender oil, Calendula oil, and Chamomile oil in a carrier oil like Coconut or Olive oil. Apply the blend topically on affected areas 2-3 times a day to soothe itching, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. Additionally, Oatmeal oil and Burdock root oil can also be added to enhance the treatment's effectiveness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eczema that are treated with herbal essential oils are psoriasis, rosacea, and dermatitis. Lavender oil helps soothe irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Tea tree oil has antifungal and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating acne-like symptoms. Geranium oil regulates hormones, which can help alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance-related skin issues, such as acne and rosacea. These essential oils can be used topically or blended with carrier oils for added benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with eczema because of its powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as terpinen-4-ol, work to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with eczema. Additionally, its antiseptic properties help to combat infection and promote healthy skin, allowing the skin to heal faster and reducing the severity of eczema symptoms. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from eczema symptoms and promote overall skin health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: It helps reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritation associated with eczema.
Linalool: It has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in reducing redness and preventing infection in eczema-prone skin.
Terpinen-4-ol: It exhibits potent antimicrobial activity, which helps control bacterial overgrowth and reduces the severity of eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm eczema symptoms.
Barks: Barks are used to make essential oils because they contain antimicrobial properties that help fight off infections and reduce inflammation associated with eczema.
Stems: Stems are used to make essential oils due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to calm and heal eczema-prone skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil has been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema. Its ability to soothe and calm the skin makes it an effective treatment for eczema sufferers. Additionally, Calendula oil promotes wound healing and tissue repair, which can help to alleviate the dry, scaly skin often associated with eczema. This natural remedy offers a gentle and non-irritating solution for managing eczema symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Isopentadecan-9-one (beta-ionone): This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema.
Isopentadecan-9-one (beta-ionone) isomer (beta-ionone isomer): This sesquiterpene has antiseptic properties that can help prevent infections and promote wound healing in eczema-prone skin.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene oxide has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that can help reduce inflammation, itching, and skin irritation associated with eczema.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties beneficial for eczema treatment.
Leaves: They are used in combination with flowers to create a more complex essential oil, with the leaves providing additional antioxidants and antimicrobial properties.
(Optional) Stems: They can also be used, although less commonly, to add a subtle variation in the essential oil's composition, but it's not as widely used as flowers and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers on a sunny day when flowers are in full bloom.
Steam distill the harvested flowers in a copper still at 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the still head using a cooled condenser at a rate of 10ml per hour.
Filter the essential oil through a 10μm filter paper to remove impurities and debris.
Store the filtered calendula essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at 20°c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil contains compounds like aloe-emodin, aloin, and aloe-annone, which work together to reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema. It also promotes skin hydration and calms irritated skin, providing relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, Aloe barbadensis oil has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections and promote healing in eczema-prone skin, making it a natural and effective remedy for managing eczema symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Glucomannan: Helps with eczema by reducing inflammation and soothing skin irritation due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Aloe-emodin: Helps with eczema by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and alleviating symptoms of inflammation.
Saponins: Helps with eczema by reducing skin inflammation and promoting wound healing due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary source of aloe vera gel, which is rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm eczema-prone skin.
Buds: Aloe vera buds are a concentrated source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote skin healing in eczema.
Roots: Aloe vera roots contain a higher concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds and other beneficial nutrients than the leaves, which can help to calm and soothe eczema-prone skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica essential oils helps with eczema because it is rich in anti-inflammatory compounds and antioxidants. The oil's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin. Additionally, the oil's moisturizing properties help to hydrate and calm the skin, promoting healing and regeneration. As a result, Urtica dioica essential oils can provide relief from eczema symptoms, reducing redness, itching, and discomfort associated with this skin condition. Its natural properties make it a popular natural remedy for eczema sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the redness and swelling associated with eczema.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Urtica dioica have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing eczema symptoms.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation, itching, and skin irritation associated with eczema.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for making essential oils due to their high content of allergenic compounds and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems are also widely used as they contain a significant amount of urtic acid and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate eczema symptoms.
Roots: The roots are used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe and calm eczema-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 1 kg of urtica dioica leaves and stems with clean scissors or pruning shears.
Steam distill the plant material at 100°c for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and sediment.
Store the final urtica dioica essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale essential oils helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil, also known as comfrey oil, contains allantoin, which has been shown to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. This makes it an effective natural remedy for alleviating eczema symptoms, such as redness and itching. Additionally, the oil's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the skin, reducing dryness and irritation associated with eczema. This natural approach can provide relief and promote healthy skin.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help with eczema by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling associated with the condition.
Flavonoids: Contribute to the relief of eczema symptoms by possessing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help soothe and calm the skin.
Alkaloids: Assist in alleviating eczema symptoms through their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory actions, which help regulate the body's immune response and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in allantoin, which helps to soothe and protect skin, making them ideal for treating eczema.
Flowers: They contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and itching associated with eczema.
Roots: They are a good source of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to calm and heal eczema-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh symphytum officinale leaves and flowers from a clean and pesticide-free area.
Steam distill the harvested material at 100°c for 2 hours to obtain the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
Label and date the bottle and store it in a cool place away from direct sunlight for up to 1 year.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with eczema because its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties soothe and calm irritated skin. The oil's ability to reduce redness and swelling provides relief from eczema symptoms. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate eczema. Lavender oil's calming effects also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can trigger eczema flare-ups, making it a popular natural remedy for managing eczema symptoms. Its moisturizing properties also help to hydrate and nourish the skin.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema.
Linalyl acetate: As a terpene, it possesses anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that aid in soothing and protecting the skin from eczema symptoms.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation, calm the skin, and promote healing in eczema-prone areas.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used to make essential oils for eczema because they contain a high concentration of calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Flowers are used to make essential oils for eczema because they contain a high amount of soothing and antiseptic properties that can help alleviate eczema symptoms.

Stems: Stems are used to make essential oils for eczema because they contain a significant amount of antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place for 1-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried flowers at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separator.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any remaining impurities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis essential oils helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's saponins and flavonoids soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling. Saponaria officinalis essential oils also exhibit antifungal properties, which can help combat infections that contribute to eczema symptoms. Furthermore, its moisturizing and emollient properties help to lock in moisture, promoting healthy skin and reducing the appearance of dry, itchy patches associated with eczema.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema.
Phenolic acids: They possess potent antioxidant properties, which can neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, a common underlying factor in eczema.
Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing symptoms of eczema.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Saponaria officinalis are used to make essential oils for eczema due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Saponaria officinalis are used to make essential oils for eczema due to their antiseptic and soothing properties.
Roots: The roots of Saponaria officinalis are used to make essential oils for eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita essential oils helps with eczema because it has potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The oils' ability to reduce redness and swelling can help alleviate discomfort and promote healing. Chamomilla recutita's calming effects can also ease emotional stress, a known trigger for eczema flare-ups. Additionally, its antiseptic properties can help prevent infection and promote a healthy skin environment, making it a popular natural remedy for eczema sufferers. This oil promotes a balanced skin ecosystem.
remedy_constituents: Alpha-bisabolol: Helps with eczema by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, which can aid in soothing and calming irritated skin.
Apigenin: Helps with eczema by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing redness and swelling, and also exhibiting antioxidant properties to protect the skin from damage.
Bisabolol oxide: Helps with eczema by exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to prevent infections and reduce inflammation in the skin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of apigenin and bisabolol, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties beneficial for eczema.
Leaves: Leaves are also used to make essential oils, as they contain a smaller amount of the beneficial compounds found in the flowers, but still offer some therapeutic benefits.
Stems: Some producers use the stems of Chamomilla recutita to make essential oils, although they may contain lower concentrations of beneficial compounds compared to flowers and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of freshly dried flower heads of chamomilla recutita at dawn when dew is still present.
Steam distill the harvested flowers at 100°c for 2 hours to release essential oil and water.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separator or decanter within 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a 10 micron filter paper to remove any impurities immediately.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils helps with eczema because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The oil, derived from the St. John's Wort plant, contains hyperforin and hypericin, which have been shown to reduce redness and itching associated with eczema. By promoting skin healing and reducing inflammation, Hypericum perforatum essential oils can help soothe and calm irritated skin. Its natural antiseptic properties also prevent infection and promote a healthy skin environment, making it an effective natural remedy for eczema sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema.
N-alkylamides: These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antipruritic (anti-itching) properties, which can provide relief from eczema symptoms.
Phenolic acids: They exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, making them a popular choice for eczema treatment.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, the flowers help reduce inflammation and promote skin regeneration, making them beneficial for eczema sufferers.
Stems: Containing a range of terpenoids and flavonoids, the stems contribute to the anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of the essential oil, which helps alleviate eczema symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers at peak bloom when the plant is in full sun.
Steam distill the flowers for 2 hours to separate essential oil from plant material using a 1:5 ratio.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from water through decantation and filtration techniques slowly.
Transfer the essential oil to a dark glass bottle for storage away from heat and light sources immediately.
Label the bottle with the date harvested and the concentration of essential oil as a reference for future use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with eczema because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in soothing and calming irritated skin. Its natural compounds, including alkylamides and caffeic acid, work to reduce inflammation and promote skin health. Additionally, Echinacea's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate eczema symptoms. By balancing the skin's natural pH and reducing inflammation, Echinacea purpurea essential oils provide relief and promote healthy skin, making it a popular natural remedy for eczema sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with eczema by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Cichoric acid: A phenolic compound that exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and calm the skin, reducing eczema symptoms.
Isoflavones: These flavonoids have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system and reduce the severity of eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Roots: They are used to create essential oils that promote skin regeneration and reduce inflammation in eczema-prone skin.
Leaves: They contain compounds that can help reduce redness and itching associated with eczema, making them a popular choice for essential oil blends.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100g of echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves at their peak potency when in full bloom.
Steam distill the harvested material using a distillation apparatus for 4-6 hours to extract oil.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a separator for 30 minutes.
Filter the oil through a diatomaceous earth filter to remove impurities and debris for 10 minutes.
Store the final oil in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid to preserve its quality.
